text: A two-centimeter vertical incision is made through the umbilicus. The surgeon seals and cuts the bilateral round ligaments using a five-millimeter unseal device.
The surgeon lifts and cuts the uterovesicle fold of peritoneum, then seals and cuts the bilateral ligamentum proprium and tuba uterinas.
The surgeon achieves hemostasis to control back bleeding and uses a sponge to push the bladder downward, creating space for further dissection.
The surgeon seals and cuts the lateral uterine arteries. Using a monopolar hook and end seal, the cervical-vaginal junction is incised in a circular manner, assisted by the uterine manipulator.

Following the hysterectomy, the surgeon performs bilateral salpingectomy, removing both fallopian tubes. The vaginal cuff is then sutured intracorporeally, completing the closure of the surgical site.
